div.p: SELECT name, age FROM users WHERE status = 'it''s active' AND id > 42 ^col1 ^col68

g.text: 15 tokens + EOF — zero-copy (start pointers into original string)

div.p: Parser calls peek() / advance() on Token[]. Never sees raw characters — only typed tokens. Abstraction boundary: tokenizer changes (new operators, new keywords) are invisible to parser.

g.text: 0x08 | const char* | start // ptr into SQL string
Total: 24 bytes (zero-copy — no heap alloc per token)

div.p: -42 emits TOK_MINUS + TOK_INTEGER(42) NOT a single negative literal. Parser applies unary minus. Reason: 10-42 must be 10, -, 42 — not 10, -42.
Case-insensitive lookup: SELECT, select, SeLeCt all emit TOK_KEYWORD. Linear scan of ~50 keywords (O(1) amortized — fits in cache).
SQL escapes ' by doubling: '' FSM uses MAYBE_END state. Token keeps raw lexeme including quotes. Parser strips + unescapes when it needs the actual value.
